textRun.content: Below is the exact disaster-recovery procedure to restore Clawbot into a brand-new VM in under 90 seconds. This is a real recovery drill — not theory — using the backup system currently in place.
Goal: Fully restore Clawbot from Google Drive backups into a fresh VM.
Estimated time: 60–90 seconds after VM creation.
Create a standard Ubuntu VM (same as current environment):
sudo apt install -y rclone tar python3
Paste the previously generated OAuth token when prompted.
(or restart your OpenClaw service if running via systemd/pm2.)
Nothing needs to be rebuilt manually.
Backups → Tarball → Google Drive → New VM → Extract → Run
No reconfiguration. No data loss. No downtime beyond VM spin-up.
Run this drill once every few months to ensure recovery confidence.
If you can restore once successfully, disaster recovery stops being a risk and becomes a routine.
